best_agent_match.title: Unbounded returndata copies let a malicious target brick execution with a return-data bomb

rejected_candidates.title: Untrusted external calls can recursively reenter the public strategy entrypoint
rejected_candidates.reason: Reentrancy requires one of the fixed hard-coded callees to actively call back into `executeOnOpportunity()`, but the verifier source alone does not show such behavior; this is too speculative and largely overlaps the existing unrestricted-entry and blind-call findings.

rejected_candidates.title: Profit signals are spoofable because anyone can inject ETH/WETH during execution
rejected_candidates.reason: Masking a loss here requires the attacker to donate ETH/WETH to the verifier, which subsidizes rather than extracts value, and no on-chain payout or privileged state transition depends on the local balance-delta heuristic.

rejected_candidates.title: Unbounded returndata copies let a malicious target brick execution with a return-data bomb
rejected_candidates.reason: A returndata bomb would require a malicious/nonstandard callee at one of the fixed addresses; on the intended chain those endpoints are canonical, while arbitrary-contract-at-fixed-address risk on the wrong chain is already covered by F-005.
